name: Invisible Funnel
origin: Created by Daegan Smith. Training/event is positioned as FREE upfront; attendee registers and enters a credit card, but is only charged if they liked the content.

whenToUse.summary: Use when the offer is a paid training/event but the client wants maximum registration volume and is willing to use a reverse-risk 'only charged if you liked it' billing model, then soft-sell a high-ticket backend.
goals: Maximize webinar/event registrations by removing the pay-wall friction upfront
Convert on the strength of content quality, not the sales page
Feed a high-ticket backend application after the event
signals: Client says 'I want people to pay only if they get value'
High-quality training/event with strong content confidence
Wants both volume (free frame) AND revenue (conditional charge)
Plans a high-ticket coaching/consulting backend offer
antiSignals: Pure free lead magnet with no charge intent (use Squeeze/Lead Magnet)
Standard paid webinar with normal checkout (use Webinar Funnel)
Client uncomfortable storing cards for delayed billing

pageStructure.purpose: Get the visitor to register AND enter a credit card under the 'only billed if you liked it' frame.
keyElements: Curiosity 'Big Promise' headline + sub-headline naming the result
Plain-language billing explainer block: 'Enter your card to reserve a seat. Do nothing and your card is billed in 2 days — if you didn't love it, just tell us and you owe nothing.'
Fascination bullets teasing what will be taught (the 3 secrets)

pageStructure.purpose: Confirm the seat, restate the billing terms, and pre-frame the event so they show up and pay attention.
keyElements: Short indoctrination video from the Attractive Character
Re-state of the pay-if-you-like-it terms (transparency builds trust)
What to bring / why to attend live

pageStructure.purpose: Deliver the actual training/webinar so the content earns the charge.
keyElements: Perfect Webinar body: Intro hook → 3 Secrets → Stack → soft transition to backend
Soft-sell CTA to the high-ticket application near the close

pageStructure.purpose: Run the conditional-billing logic (charge after 2 days unless they cancel) and route happy attendees into a high-ticket application.
keyElements: 'Loved it? Do nothing.' / 'Not for you? Cancel here' branching
Soft-sell application CTA for the higher-ticket backend offer
Application form that gets the prospect to qualify/sell themselves
Calendar booking for the qualification call

scripts: Event body = Perfect Webinar (Intro/Hook → 3 Secrets → Stack → Close)
Registration page = Copywriting Secrets Big-Promise headline + fascination bullets + risk-reversal block (the conditional-billing frame IS the risk reversal)
Indoctrination + event open = Epiphany Bridge origin story
High-ticket application copy = Star/Story/Solution take-away frame
copyFramework.notes: The conditional-billing language is the load-bearing copy element. Keep it transparent and repeated on pages 1, 2, and 4 — trust is what makes attendees allow the charge.

skill44Widgets: Order form widget configured for authorize-now / capture-later (delayed billing)
Calendar/booking widget for the closer call
automations: Tag on register; SMS/email reminder sequence pre-event
Post-event delay timer (2 days) → capture charge unless 'cancel' tag set
Cancel link sets 'invisible-cancel' tag and voids capture
On application submit → notify closer + booking confirmation
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 builds the 4 pages and wires the payment element for delayed capture; Skill 44 generates the registration form, the application form, the calendar booking widget, and the cancel/keep survey.
